_comment: Runnable (system-1) conformance spec for ai-recommendations, projected from .claude/contracts/ai-recommendations/INTERFACE_CONTRACT.md '## 3. Conformance spec' into the flat schema interface_conformance.py executes ({method,path,path_params,query,body,required}). The contract's embedded spec is the human-readable truth; this is its machine-runnable projection (same precedent as conformance/api_metrics.json). The embedded block is NOT directly runnable by the tool (heading is '## 3. Conformance spec' so the tool's '## Conformance spec' regex misses it, and its nested request/response schema differs from the flat schema the tool reads) — flagged for a GATE Z amendment. GATE Z AMENDMENT (2026-06-25, user-accounts): POST /api/recommendation/{ticker} is now AUTH-GATED outermost (the 'ask AI' call requires a signed-in session) — an anonymous probe now correctly returns 403, so this endpoint can no longer be conformance-checked by the cookieless tool and was REMOVED from this anonymous runnable sweep. Its emitted shape is unchanged for a signed-in caller and is verified under user-accounts (signed-in 200 path + the gated 403 path) by the backend runtime proofs + FE tests. The remaining three endpoints (export floor, status read, personas) stay anonymous-200 and are still checked here. The POST sent override:true so the gated_off short-circuit was bypassed and `strategy` was deterministically a full object under the no-key STUB provider. Uses SPY (a liquid ticker that must be fetched once before running, e.g. curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/ticker/SPY).
